messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Build error in nebbiolo1 for package BgeeCall
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Build error in nebbiolo1 for package BgeeCall
messages.body_plain:
Hello,

I am the maintainer of the BgeeCall package. There is an issue in the build of the package on nebbiolo1 since at least the last Bioconductor release.
The error comes from the vignette when I try to load data from the AnnotationHub package. I am not able to reproduce the error locally. Do you have any idea what the problem could be?

block of code that produce the error:

library(AnnotationHub)
library(rtracklayer)
ah <- AnnotationHub()
ah_resources <- query(ah, c("Ensembl", "Caenorhabditis elegans", "84"))
annotation_object <- ah_resources[["AH50789"]]
# remove MtDNA not tag as C. elegans genome
annotation_object <- dropSeqlevels(annotation_object, "MtDNA", "coarse")
transcriptome_object <- import.2bit(ah_resources[["AH50453"]])

The error message is :


Quitting from lines 94-102 [unnamed-chunk-4] (bgeecall-manual.Rmd)
Error: processing vignette 'bgeecall-manual.Rmd' failed with diagnostics:
UCSC library operation failed

Best Regards,

Julien Wollbrett

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Call for Bioconductor course facilitators
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats <http://instats.org> is seeking to offer a range Bioconductor
courses running anywhere from 1 - 5 days in length, and we are currently
searching for a facilitator or facilitators for these.

Instats (the Institute for Statistical and Data Science) partners with
leading researchers, universities, and professional associations --
including the American Statistical Association -- to offer research methods
training through livestreamed and on-demand workshops to a global audience. One
nice feature of Instats is that we are all researchers ourselves, so we
never seek to own our partners' IP and we are happy for our partners to
offer free or fee-based courses (for any fee-based courses we share all
revenue equitably). We also believe in academic freedom, so you can offer
the content you want, in the format you prefer, and do it on your schedule.
After a livestreamed course, we are able to host the content for on-demand
access for streaming across the globe, and we are happy to allow all of
your institution?s students and researchers free access to any of your
fee-based content. This would allow you to use Instats as a free research
training platform internally, while generating revenue whenever such
content sells outside your institution, if you'd like this option.

If you have a PhD and would like to have a chat about facilitating a
Bioconductor course, it would be great to meet for a quick chat. Please let
me know what might work for you and a member of our team would be happy to
schedule a Zoom meeting at a time convenient for you.


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
*instats.org* <http://instats.org>
<http://instats.org>

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Timeout on package builder: R CMD build-specific issue
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Timeout on package builder: R CMD build-specific issue
messages.body_plain:
Hi,

Recently my package (universalmotif) has suddenly started timing out on both release and devel builds for all platforms (https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/devel/bioc-LATEST/universalmotif/), stopping at the "creating vignettes" stage. I've determined that the issue is this particular line in one of the vignettes which hangs indefinitely:

https://github.com/bjmt/universalmotif/blob/master/vignettes/MotifComparisonAndPvalues.Rmd#L27

However, I cannot understand why... Compiling the vignette from within R on my laptop with rmarkdown::render works fine, and the code runs instantly by itself in my terminal. My package hasn't been updated with any code changes recently, and as far as I can tell neither has any dependency which touches this part of the code.

Does anyone happen to know why R CMD build elicits such different behaviour compared to interactively running the code? I can confirm that the hang occurs locally when using R CMD build, so it's not specific to the Bioc machines as far as I can tell. Unfortunately I haven't had any success finding any information about this particular kind of issue.

Thanks in advance,

Benjamin

messages.body_plain:
Hi,

I am the maintainer of the cfTools package. I found cfTools have build
errors on palomino7/8 and kunpeng2 for BioC 3.19 and 3.20, which appear to
be caused by an environment issue related to a Python dependency. This has
happened before and was solved. Could you help me solve this again? And is
there a way to avoid this error in future when there is a new release?

Thank you very much!

Best,
Ran

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Build error for cfTools
messages.body_plain:
Hi Ran,

Do you remember how it was solved before (for kunpeng2) ?

https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/tensorflow does not provide linux-aarch64
build even for 2.17.0 in the conda-forge channel. Your package uses 2.10.0.
The anaconda channel does provide it -
https://anaconda.org/anaconda/tensorflow, but AFAIK using this channel is
non-free.

The builder setup docs list tensorflow as a PIP dependency:
https://github.com/Bioconductor/BBS/blob/devel/Ubuntu-files/22.04/pip_pkgs.txt#L8

biocbuild at kunpeng2 ~> pip install tensorflow
Defaulting to user installation because normal site-packages is not
writeable
Requirement already satisfied: tensorflow in
/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages (2.12.0)
Requirement already satisfied: tensorflow-cpu-aws==2.12.0 in
/usr/local/lib64/python3.9/site-packages (from tensorflow) (2.12.0)

I.e. tensorflow 2.12.0 is installed via pip, but conda does not use pip
packages.

Regards,
Martin

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] support.bioconductor.org very slow
messages.body_plain:
Thanks to you both and sorry for the inconvenience, Gordon.  We have been
working on reducing cloud expenses in various directions.  This has been
important
because of an across-the-board budget cut at NIH -- we lost 17% of our
expected
budget.  I do not think there was an intentional reduction in support site
responsiveness,
but it may be a side-effect of other intentional changes.  I hope we can
diagnose and
repair promptly.

For what it is worth, right now I am seeing a long latency in Boston.  We
can introduce
monitoring once this bad behavior is resolved.

While we are on the topic, I will mention that we are *assessing* the
process of transitioning
our support site infrastructure to Discourse.  This would be important
insofar as we no longer
have access to technical support for our fork of Biostars.  Our small group
is evaluating how
to transition the entire corpus from the current system to Discourse, to
minimize disruption
of access to historical content.

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor 3.20 Release Schedule
messages.body_plain:
The release date for Bioc 3.20 is tentatively scheduled for Wednesday October 30th. The 3.20 release will use R-4.4.  The following highlights important deadlines for the release.

Important deadlines can be found on the website:
https://bioconductor.org/developers/release-schedule/


As a Quick Summary:

Friday September 20: New package submission deadline. Packages will still need to pass the formal review process to be included in Release 3.20. We will try to have at least an initial review of the package if submitted by this date. Packages submitted after this date are not guaranteed to be reviewed; they will be reviewed time pending. Package reviewers are volunteer and have a limited capacity for review.


Wednesday October 9: No API changes to devel 3.20. Devel 3.20 is considered a release candidate, and changes should be limited to bug corrections only.


Tuesday October 15: Current Release 3.19 Freeze. No changes will be accepted to the Bioconductor 3.19 branch after this date.


Wednesday October 23: Newly submitted packages need to be accepted by this date to be included in the release


Friday October 25: Deadline for packages to be passing R CMD install/build/check on Bioconductor builders.


Tuesday October 29: Devel 3.20 branch is frozen to create release branch.


Wednesday October 30: Release Bioconductor 3.20


Post Release: Dockers and Single Package Builder Updates, Mirror updates

Cheers,
Lori Shepherd - Kern on behalf of the Core Team



Lori Shepherd - Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Build fails for Moonlight2R
messages.body_plain:
Dear maintainers,

we've been having a build fail on Palomino7 (Windows builder) for our Moonlight2R package release branch (3.19), one of our tests fails. I cannot reproduce this fail locally and looks like it might be an issue with data from AnnotationHub:

?? Error ('test-DMA.R:14:1'): (code run outside of `test_that()`) ??????????????

Error: failed to load resource

  name: AH14150

  title: hg19ToHg38.over.chain.gz

  reason: expected 11 elements in header, got 1, on line 8

Backtrace:

     ?

  1. ??Moonlight2R::DMA(...) at test-DMA.R:14:1

  2.   ??Moonlight2R::LiftMAF(Infile = DEGs_mut_annotated_19, Current_Build = "GRCh37")

  3.     ??ah[["AH14150"]]

  4.     ??ah[["AH14150"]]

  5.       ??AnnotationHub (local) .local(x, i, j = j, ...)

  6.         ??AnnotationHub:::.Hub_get1(x[idx], force = force, verbose = verbose)

  7.           ??base::tryCatch(...)

  8.             ??base (local) tryCatchList(expr, classes, parentenv, handlers)

  9.               ??base (local) tryCatchOne(expr, names, parentenv, handlers[[1L]])

 10.                 ??value[[3L]](cond)

Similarly, in the same Moonlight2R package and devel version (3.20), we have a fail on Kunpeng2 in one of the examples which might depend on the runner (or on the fact that the NCBI ftp might have been down), as I cannot reproduce it locally either:

> dataGEO <-  getDataGEO(GEOobject = "GSE15641", platform = "GPL96")
Error in open.connection(x, "rb") :
  Timeout was reached: [ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov] Resolving timed out after 10000 milliseconds
Calls: getDataGEO ... getAndParseGSEMatrices -> getDirListing -> <Anonymous> -> read_html.default
Execution halted

For the latter, it is possible we just might need to wait for the next test round, but I thought I?d mention just in case.

If you could please advise or have a look, it would be very appreciated.

Thank you and kind regards,

Matteo Tiberti

Danish Cancer Institute
Strandboulevarden 49
DK-2100 Copenhagen
Telephone: +45 35 25 73 07
? a part of the Danish Cancer Society
